item.title: How you can use your laptop as a second monitor for your PC
item.itunes:title: How you can use your laptop as a second monitor for your PC
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
If you're someone who multi-tasks a lot on your PC, you know you can never have enough space for all your apps, and working on a single screen can be a huge hinderance. Buying a second monitor is probably the best option, but what if you don't want to do that right now? The good news is you can use your laptop as a second screen for your main computer, and you don't even need a third-party app for it. Here's how it works.

Use your Windows laptop as a wireless display

Windows 11 comes with an app called Connect, which is hidden by default. By installing this app, your laptop becomes a wireless display, as long as it supports Miracast (which most Windows laptops do). Here's what you need to do:

  1. On the laptop that will be your second monitor, open the Settings app.
  2. Navigate to the System section and scroll down to Optional features.

    On some older versions of Windows, optional features may be in the Apps or Apps and features section.

  3. Click View features next to Add an optional feature.
  4. Scroll down to find the Wireless display option and check the box next to it.
  5. Click Next.
  6. Click Add.

Now, just wait for your PC to finish installing the feature, and your laptop will be ready to act as a wireless display. This may take a while, so be patient. Once it's done, you should see a Wireless Display app (also called Connect, in some versions of Windows) in your Start menu. Follow these steps:

  1. Open the Wireless Display app on the laptop you're using as a second monitor
  2. On your main PC, press Windows + K to search for wireless displays.
  3. Select your laptop's name from the list of options.
  4. Once the connection is established, you'll see your screen on your laptop.
  5. If you want to extend your screen, choose the Extend option in the connection flyout on your main PC (Windows defaults to the Duplicate option).

    In the connection flyout, you can also check the box to Allow mouse, keyboard, and other input from this device, if you'd like to be able to interact with your main PC using the secondary laptop.

That's all you need to do to use your laptop as a wireless display for your main PC. It's a fairly straightforward process, though we should mention that wireless connections like this can sometimes be unstable, so you may experience some visual glitches and delays this way.

Make projection easier

If you don't want to have to open the Wireless Display app every time, you can change the projection settings on your laptop to make it more easily discoverable. To do this:

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Go to System and then choose Projecting to this PC.
  3. Use the dropdown menu under Some Windows and Android devices can project to this PC when you say it's OK and choose either Available everywhere or Available everywhere on secure networks.

    Beware that this may enable other people to connect to your PC to use as a wireless display, though you still have to accept connection requests.

  4. If your laptop isn't connected to power when you do this, you may also want to disable the option labeled This PC can be discovered only when it's plugged into a power source. This will allow it to be detected even when it's on battery power.

Use a MacBook as a wireless display

If you're a Mac user and you also happen to have two Mac devices, you can also use one of them as a wireless display thanks to AirPlay Display. For this to work, you'll need both devices to be connected to the same network and logged in with the same Apple ID. With that established, simply follow these steps:

  1. On your main Mac, open the Control Center from the menu bar at the top of the screen.
  2. Choose Screen Mirroring.
  3. Your MacBook or secondary Mac should appear as an option to connect to. Click it.

Alternatively, you can add a laptop as an external display by going to the Apple menu and then choosing Displays. From there, click Add Display, and your secondary Mac should show up just as with the steps above.

Improve your productivity in a pinch

Having more screens is a great way to stay more productive, but we don't all have a second monitor at hand, so using a laptop as a makeshift solution is a totally valid solution. With these steps, you can easily get started and expand your workspace.
item.description:
If you're someone who multi-tasks a lot on your PC, you know you can never have enough space for all your apps, and working on a single screen can be a huge hinderance. Buying a second monitor is probably the best option, but what if you don't want to do that right now? The good news is you can use your laptop as a second screen for your main computer, and you don't even need a third-party app for it. Here's how it works.

Use your Windows laptop as a wireless display

Windows 11 comes with an app called Connect, which is hidden by default. By installing this app, your laptop becomes a wireless display, as long as it supports Miracast (which most Windows laptops do). Here's what you need to do:

  1. On the laptop that will be your second monitor, open the Settings app.
  2. Navigate to the System section and scroll down to Optional features.

    On some older versions of Windows, optional features may be in the Apps or Apps and features section.

  3. Click View features next to Add an optional feature.
  4. Scroll down to find the Wireless display option and check the box next to it.
  5. Click Next.
  6. Click Add.

Now, just wait for your PC to finish installing the feature, and your laptop will be ready to act as a wireless display. This may take a while, so be patient. Once it's done, you should see a Wireless Display app (also called Connect, in some versions of Windows) in your Start menu. Follow these steps:

  1. Open the Wireless Display app on the laptop you're using as a second monitor
  2. On your main PC, press Windows + K to search for wireless displays.
  3. Select your laptop's name from the list of options.
  4. Once the connection is established, you'll see your screen on your laptop.
  5. If you want to extend your screen, choose the Extend option in the connection flyout on your main PC (Windows defaults to the Duplicate option).

    In the connection flyout, you can also check the box to Allow mouse, keyboard, and other input from this device, if you'd like to be able to interact with your main PC using the secondary laptop.

That's all you need to do to use your laptop as a wireless display for your main PC. It's a fairly straightforward process, though we should mention that wireless connections like this can sometimes be unstable, so you may experience some visual glitches and delays this way.

Make projection easier

If you don't want to have to open the Wireless Display app every time, you can change the projection settings on your laptop to make it more easily discoverable. To do this:

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Go to System and then choose Projecting to this PC.
  3. Use the dropdown menu under Some Windows and Android devices can project to this PC when you say it's OK and choose either Available everywhere or Available everywhere on secure networks.

    Beware that this may enable other people to connect to your PC to use as a wireless display, though you still have to accept connection requests.

  4. If your laptop isn't connected to power when you do this, you may also want to disable the option labeled This PC can be discovered only when it's plugged into a power source. This will allow it to be detected even when it's on battery power.

Use a MacBook as a wireless display

If you're a Mac user and you also happen to have two Mac devices, you can also use one of them as a wireless display thanks to AirPlay Display. For this to work, you'll need both devices to be connected to the same network and logged in with the same Apple ID. With that established, simply follow these steps:

  1. On your main Mac, open the Control Center from the menu bar at the top of the screen.
  2. Choose Screen Mirroring.
  3. Your MacBook or secondary Mac should appear as an option to connect to. Click it.

Alternatively, you can add a laptop as an external display by going to the Apple menu and then choosing Displays. From there, click Add Display, and your secondary Mac should show up just as with the steps above.

Improve your productivity in a pinch

Having more screens is a great way to stay more productive, but we don't all have a second monitor at hand, so using a laptop as a makeshift solution is a totally valid solution. With these steps, you can easily get started and expand your workspace.

Having more screens is a great way to stay more productive, but we don't all have a second monitor at hand, so using a laptop as a makeshift solution is a totally valid solution. With these steps, you can easily get started and expand your workspace.

item.title: A major feature will be retired from Microsoft 365 soon
item.itunes:title: A major feature will be retired from Microsoft 365 soon
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Microsoft 365 is removing its Feed feature in November.
  • The change aims to streamline the user experience and make better use of space.
  • Users have until November 1st to transition from the Feed feature to the newer Recommended feed.

How often do you use the Feed feature on Microsoft 365? If you've never heard of or used Feed until today, don't worry too much about learning how to use it. The Redmond giant has suddenly confirmed that the Feed feature is getting the axe, only two years after it first arrived on the productivity suite.

 Related
FlexiSpot C7 Ergonomic Office Chair review: For those who want lumbar support, and a lot of it

The FlexiSpot C7 Ergonomic Chair is designed to reduce back pain and facilitate good posture, but its aggressive lumbar support won't be for everyone.

Microsoft 365's Feed feature will be removed in November

As announced on the Microsoft 365 Blog, the Feeds feature first debuted in 2022 to give users a summary of everything that was going on in their workspace. However, Microsoft slowly relegated that duty to a newer "Recommended" feed that appears when you boot up Microsoft 365. As such, the company doesn't see much point in keeping Feeds around for much longer:

As part of our ongoing efforts to streamline and enhance user experiences, we will be retiring the Feed feature [...] on the Microsoft 365 app. This change will affect web endpoints (www.microsoft365.com, www.office.com) and the Windows app (“Microsoft 365 (Office)”).

I can see why Microsoft made the change; in the comparison screenshots between the old and the new (as seen above), removing the Feeds tab frees up space on the sidebar and gives the Home tab a little more use. Instead of having to rummage around Microsoft 365 for all of your updates, you get a palatable stream of updates the moment you boot up the app.

However, there are bound to be people who enjoyed Feed's presentation of a list of updates, over the Recommended section's slideshow-style presentation. Still, if you're a big fan of Feed, you have until November 1st to say your last goodbyes and adjust your workflow for the update. In the meantime, did you know that Office 2024 has just been released?

  • Office Home 2024

    The essentials to get it all done. Office Home 2024 is for students and families who want classic Office apps including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint for Windows 11 and Windows 10. A one-time purchase installed on 1 PC or Mac for use at home or school.

    $149.99 at Microsoft
  • Office Home & Business 2024

    The essentials to get it all done. Office Home and Business 2024 is for families and small businesses who want classic Office apps and email. It includes Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook for Windows 11 and Windows 10. A one-time purchase installed on one PC or Mac for use at home or work.

    $249.99 at Microsoft

item.title: 7 reasons Obsidian is better than Notion for productivity seekers
item.itunes:title: 7 reasons Obsidian is better than Notion for productivity seekers
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
While Notion has long been the favorite productivity app for power users, a strong competitor, Obsidian, has recently come into the spotlight. You shouldn’t mistake Obsidian for another note-taking app. It’s an offline solution that offers a unique approach to knowledge management, uncovers hidden insights, and goes beyond just basic notes and databases. In this article, I'll outline the main reasons why Obsidian could be a superior option to Notion for those looking to enhance their productivity.

 Related
My journey from Notion to Obsidian - why I switched and never looked back

Obsidian took me to the next level and I escaped the limitations of Notion

1

7 Offline-first solution

Offline support is one of the biggest reasons for choosing Obsidian over Notion. The latter requires an active internet connection, without which you cannot access or edit your workspace. On the other hand, obsidian lets you create and save your vaults on your device so that they’re always within reach, even without network connectivity.

Offline support also leads to faster performance. With Notion, performance takes a hit when you deal with a large set of databases and blocks. There is no such issue with Obsidian. This seemingly simple difference has profound implications for your productivity.

6 Markdown support

Obsidian has a Markdown-based editor built-in. Markdown is a popular language that uses hashtags (#), asterisks (*), and other special characters to format your notes like a pro. It’s a universal language supported by many popular applications, like Google Docs, iA Writer, Ulysses, Joplin, and more. Although mastering the Markdown language can be challenging at first, it becomes extremely enjoyable to use once you overcome the initial learning curve.

By default, Obsidian saves your files in .md extension. You can also open these files in other applications. Unlike Notion, you aren’t restricted to the company’s workspace only.

 Related
Here are some Markdown tips and tricks to improve your note taking in Obsidian

Unlock the power of Markdown in Obsidian with these expert tips

5 Cloud storage flexibility

Obsidian also offers flexibility when it comes to cloud syncing. For example, you can store your vault notes offline, carry them on an external storage device of your choice, and pick any cloud storage platform like Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, and more to sync your notes across devices. There is an option to store your notes on a self-hosted platform like Nextcloud. Overall, you have complete freedom, control, and ownership over your vaults.

It's entirely up to the user how they wish to store and sync their Obsidian notes across platforms. You can even pick a Sync add-on for $5 per month and use the company’s servers to store and sync notes across devices (it also comes with a neat advantage that I will discuss later in the post).

4 The sheer amount of customization options

Customization is where Obsidian truly shines over its rivals, including Notion. With Notion, you can only switch between dark and light themes, and pick from a few different font styles. In contrast, Obsidian has a dedicated theme store built-in. You can simply head to Settings > Appearance and open a theme store.

You can glance over a long list of light and dark themes with different accent colors, background shades, and text styles for your Obsidian vault. There is also an option to assign and customize hotkeys (keyboard shortcuts) for your frequently used functions in Obsidian. When you have dozens of core and community plugins installed, you also have the flexibility to disable irrelevant ones to keep your workflow smooth.

3 Robust plugin store

Compared to Notion, Obsidian may feel barebone at first glance. And here is where the plugin store comes into play. Since Obsidian has an active community, third-party developers have chimed in to fill in the gaps in your vault. For example, Obsidian doesn’t support Kanban boards by default. However, you can simply head to the plugin store, download a Kanban board plugin, and enjoy the same in your vault.

Similarly, you can enable advanced tables, calendars, tasks, day planners, and much more with the help of third-party plugins. With Notion, you are stuck with the built-in functionalities only.

 Related
10 best Obsidian plugins to supercharge your workflow

Your ideal Obsidian setup is incomplete without these plugins

1

2 Built-in Canvas and Graph views

Notion only lets you interlink relevant notes. Obsidian takes the entire experience to the next level with a built-in Canvas and Graph view. They offer unique visual ways to interact with your notes, significantly boosting productivity for visual thinkers and those who thrive on seeing connections.

You can visualize your hidden connections and knowledge network, navigate your vault with ease, and explore flexible notes organization. If you want to learn more, check out our dedicated post on creating and using Graph view in Obsidian.

 Related
How to visualize your notes in Obsidian with Graph view

Mapping your mind and unlocking insights with Obsidian’s Graph view

1 End-to-end encryption for peace of mind

As mentioned, if you choose to go with the Sync add-on ($5 monthly), Obsidian enables end-to-end encryption for your notes. If you frequently store confidential and private notes in your vault, make sure to opt for the paid add-on to unlock peace of mind. Notion doesn’t support E2EE.

 Related
5 best private note-taking apps offering ultimate security

Fort Knox for your thoughts and ideas

1

Obsidian reigns supreme for productivity

As you can see from the list above, Obsidian surely is a better choice than Notion for personal use. Notion’s strength mostly lies in complex project management, real-time collaboration, and flexibility due to a block editor. Obsidian allows you to control your data with local storage and the flexibility of Markdown support, and also offers a wide range of plugins to extend your vault’s functionality in no time. Check out our dedicated post if you want to use Obsidian to create a PKM system.

They offer unique visual ways to interact with your notes, significantly boosting productivity for visual thinkers and those who thrive on seeing connections.

You can visualize your hidden connections and knowledge network, navigate your vault with ease, and explore flexible notes organization. If you want to learn more, check out our dedicated post on creating and using Graph view in Obsidian.

 Related
How to visualize your notes in Obsidian with Graph view

Mapping your mind and unlocking insights with Obsidian’s Graph view

1 End-to-end encryption for peace of mind

As mentioned, if you choose to go with the Sync add-on ($5 monthly), Obsidian enables end-to-end encryption for your notes. If you frequently store confidential and private notes in your vault, make sure to opt for the paid add-on to unlock peace of mind. Notion doesn’t support E2EE.

 Related
5 best private note-taking apps offering ultimate security

Fort Knox for your thoughts and ideas

1

Obsidian reigns supreme for productivity

As you can see from the list above, Obsidian surely is a better choice than Notion for personal use. Notion’s strength mostly lies in complex project management, real-time collaboration, and flexibility due to a block editor. Obsidian allows you to control your data with local storage and the flexibility of Markdown support, and also offers a wide range of plugins to extend your vault’s functionality in no time. Check out our dedicated post if you want to use Obsidian to create a PKM system.

item.title: Best 14-inch laptops in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best 14-inch laptops in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
There's a wide variety of laptop sizes today, with most popular models landing somewhere in the 13-inch to 16-inch range. Of these, 14-inch laptops arguably hit the sweet spot in size, offering the perfect balance of utility and portability. The best laptops at this size are small and light enough to carry around, but 14-inch screens are still comfortable enough for productive work. Some 14-inch laptop keyboards even accommodate a number pad, which is not something you're likely to find on smaller machines. This makes 14-inch laptops an excellent choice for students, professionals, and gamers on the go. Since the market has grown significantly in recent years, you have plenty of options to choose from. Whether you want a MacBook, a 2-in-1 convertible, a gaming laptop, or an affordable Chrome OS workhorse, we've rounded up all the best 14-inch laptops right here.

  • Source: Dell
    Dell XPS 14
    Best overall
    $1500 at Dell
  • Source: Amazon
    Acer Aspire 3 A314 Slim Laptop
    Best value
    $450 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (Gen 8)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1450 at Lenovo
  • Surface Laptop Studio 2
    Best for creators
    $1950 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12
    Best business laptop
    $1230 at Newegg
  • Source: HP
    HP ZBook Firefly 14 G10
    Best workstation
    $1299 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo Yoga 9i (2024)
    Best convertible
    $1100 at Best Buy
  • MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)
    For Mac users
    $1299 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3)
  • Acer Chromebook Spin 714 (2023)
    Best Chromebook
    $530 at Amazon

Find your next "sweet spot" sized laptop among these 14-inch options

Best overall
Dell XPS 14

One of the best options in 2024, period

9 / 10
$1500 $2100 Save $600

Dell's XPS 14 is not just one of the best 14-inch laptops, it's one of the best laptops of 2024, period. It offers up to a stunning 3.2K OLED display with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate, and Intel's latest Core Ultra processors deliver great performance for work and multitasking. There's even a discrete GPU option.

Pros
  • 3.2K 120Hz OLED display option is gorgeous
  • Great design and build quality
  • Snappy Intel Core Ultra processor
  • Optional Nvidia RTX GPU
Cons
  • Fairly heavy for its size
  • No legacy USB-A ports
$1500 at Dell $1500 at Best Buy

Dell has been at the top of the Windows laptop market in recent years thanks to its excellent XPS line. The Dell XPS 14 2024 is one of our favorite recent releases and our top pick overall among the best 14-inch laptops. With its aluminum chassis, the XPS 14 looks and feels like a premium laptop, even if its metal construction makes it noticeably heavier than other machines in this category.

That premium design extends to the 14-inch screen. It comes standard with a 1200p FHD+ panel, but we favor the gorgeous 3.2K OLED upgrade with a buttery smooth variable refresh rate of 120Hz. It also has an aspect ratio of 16:10, something that's thankfully becoming more commonplace on laptops and gives you a taller display area to work in. The Dell XPS 14 runs on Intel's latest Core Ultra 7 155H processor and has 16GB of memory in its base configuration (which you can upgrade to up to 64GB), ensuring great overall performance for work, entertainment, and multitasking. There's also an optional GeForce RTX 4050 discrete GPU upgrade, allowing you to game on this thing if you're so inclined.

Related
Dell XPS 14 (2024) review: It's just so much fun

A MacBook Pro killer

Also, a 512GB solid-state drive comes standard, which should provide ample storage for most people, but you can upgrade to as much as 4TB of storage if you need to. Ports include HDMI, USB Type-C, a microSD card reader, and two Thunderbolt 4 connections, so you've got plenty of options for hooking up peripherals such as an external display, although it lacks any USB-A ports. It's a little pricey, but if you're looking for the best all-around 14-inch laptop on the market today, the Dell XPS 14 is the one to buy.

Best value
Acer Aspire 3 A314 Slim Laptop

When all you need are the basics

The Acer Aspire 3 A314 is a solid, no-frills 14-inch laptop for shoppers on a budget. Despite its affordable price, it features a nice full HD display and solid hardware. And, while the design is a bit basic, it doesn't look or feel cheap.

Pros
  • Full HD IPS display
  • Solid specs for the price
  • Good port setup
  • Includes a sleeve
Cons
  • Build quality is nothing special
  • No numpad
$450 at Amazon

Acer is no stranger to the budget laptop market and makes a lot of solid Windows PCs and Chromebooks for users who don't want to spend a lot of money. If you just need a basic but reliable 14-inch laptop, the Acer Aspire 3 A314 fits the bill. This model is available in various configurations, all running on AMD Ryzen CPUs, but the one that hits the sweet spot for us is the Ryzen 5 7520U model with 8GB of LDDR5 RAM. On top of that, you've got a 512GB SSD, which is a generous amount of storage to see at this price.

Although it's a budget laptop, the Acer Aspire 3 A314 doesn't look or feel cheap. It has a sleek and attractive design and offers a good selection of ports, including an HDMI, a USB Type-C, and two USB Type-A ports. It also features Wi-Fi 6 wireless connectivity. It's not the thinnest laptop among our picks, but it's suitably thin at just under three-quarters of an inch. The keyboard is also nicely sized, although it lacks a number pad. Some users may prefer this for a less crowded key layout though.

The 14-inch display is a 1080p IPS panel, which is another good thing to see in this price bracket (even if the bezels are a bit chunky). Many cheaper laptops cut corners with pared-down mobile hardware and 720p screens, but the Acer Aspire 3 A314 shows that you don't have to sacrifice too much to get an affordable machine built for daily use. It even comes with a protective sleeve.

Best gaming laptop
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (Gen 8)

A great gaming machine for a low(er) price

Lenovo's Legion lineup features some very capable (and often surprisingly affordable) gaming machines. The 8th-generation Legion Slim 5 14 packs impressive performance inside a reasonably compact 14-inch frame. It's available with an AMD Ryzen 7 CPU and Nvidia RTX 40-series graphics, so you've got plenty of muscle for modern gaming.

Pros
  • Powerful for its size and price
  • Outstanding 2.8K OLED screen
  • 16:10 display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Unassuming design
Cons
  • Limited configuration options
  • No G-Sync or Thunderbolt support
$1450 at Lenovo

Gaming laptops have come a long way in recent years, and while most sport larger 15- to 17-inch displays, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 shows that you can get it done in a smaller package, too. This unassuming gaming laptop features a 14-inch 16:10 display, giving gamers a more compact and portable alternative to gaming laptops (some of which are rather large and cumbersome).

Notably, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 is configured with an AMD Ryzen 7 CPU and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 mobile GPU. Nvidia has the lion's share of the gaming laptop market, so that's no surprise, but it's got plenty of horsepower for a laptop at this price. Better still, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 comes standard with an outstanding 2.8K OLED display, which is usually the sort of upgrade you have to pay a premium for. That said, there's no G-Sync support.

 Related
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (2023) review: The 14-inch gaming laptop to beat

A balance of price, features, and performance in a thin and light laptop.

Still, with that RTX 4060 GPU and 16GB of 6400MHz DDR5 RAM, you won't be disappointed with the performance, even if the memory is soldered. This gaming laptop is more than capable of running newer games well, and they'll look great on the HDR 500 display, which features a 120Hz refresh rate for smooth sailing. Although pickings are slim in the 14-inch gaming laptop niche specifically, the Lenovo Legion 5 Slim 14 doesn't feel like it makes many compromises with its smaller size.

Best for creators
Surface Laptop Studio 2

With a unique form factor, too

$1950 $2400 Save $450

The Microsoft Surface Laptop Studio 2 is a unique take on the traditional 2-in-1 convertible laptop design. Its responsive 14.4-inch touchscreen can be positioned in several ways or even folded totally flat, letting you use the Laptop Studio 2 as a tablet. This convertible also packs very impressive hardware, including the option for a discrete Nvidia RTX GPU.

Pros
  • Incredibly versatile design
  • Great hardware
  • Discrete graphics
  • Responsive 14.4-inch touchscreen
Cons
  • Quite expensive
  • Heavy, especially for a 2-in-1
$2000 at Microsoft $2400 at Best Buy $1950 at Amazon

Microsoft's Surface Laptop series spans a huge range of features and prices, and the Studio Laptop 2 is the company's most powerful (and most expensive) Surface machine yet. This unique convertible puts a fresh spin on the standard 2-in-1 design, featuring a bright and color-accurate 14.4-inch touch display that can be pulled forward towards you instead of just flipped up. The stylus pen included with the Laptop Studio 2 now supports haptic feedback, making writing and drawing much more responsive than it was on the previous model.

The Surface Laptop Studio 2 packs impressive hardware under the hood, too. It comes standard with a 13th-generation Intel Core i7 CPU and 16GB of RAM (upgradable to 64GB). The base configuration features Intel Iris Xe integrated graphics, but it's available with an Nvidia RTX discrete GPU, which is an upgrade we'd recommend for creative professionals such as video editors and graphic designers. Combined with its excellent touch display and haptic stylus pen, a dedicated graphics card makes the Surface Laptop Studio 2 a creator's dream.

 Related
Microsoft Surface Laptop Studio 2 review: The most powerful Surface ever

The Surface Laptop Studio 2 is all about more power

Of course, as Microsoft's most powerful and feature-rich Surface device, the Laptop Studio 2 is not cheap. Pricing starts at $2,000 for the base configuration without a discrete GPU. This convertible is also rather heavy, weighing a little more than four pounds. Still, if you're looking for the best 14-inch laptop for creative work, the Surface Laptop Studio 2 is a powerful and versatile machine that can handle almost anything you'd need a computer to do.

Best business laptop
Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12

And with that iconic ThinkPad design

$1820 $2030 Save $210

The ThinkPad name has been synonymous with "business laptops" for decades now. The 12th-generation ThinkPad X1 Carbon lives up to that pedigree with its modern hardware, solid build quality, sleek and lightweight design, and iconic ThinkPad styling.

Pros
  • Premium Core Ultra performance
  • Sleek design with classic ThinkPad styling
  • 1080p Full HD webcam
  • Slim and lightweight
Cons
  • ThinkPad design is not for everyone
  • Soldered RAM
$1362 at Lenovo $1820 at Best Buy $1230 at Newegg

If you're at all familiar with business laptops, then the ThinkPad series needs no introduction. ThinkPad laptops have been a staple in offices around the world for decades, and the X1 Carbon line has helped Lenovo maintain the ThinkPad pedigree in the age of ultrabooks and 2-in-1s. The newest 12th-generation ThinkPad X1 Carbon is our pick for the best 14-inch laptop for business professionals thanks to its excellent hardware, powerful performance, and great design wrapped in the iconic styling that made the ThinkPads famous.

The ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12 comes loaded with Intel's latest Core Ultra CPUs and comes standard with 16GB of RAM (which you can upgrade to 64GB, although it's soldered), so performance is no issue. The ThinkPad X1 Carbon is a proven performer when it comes to handling heavy workloads, running demanding productivity software, and juggling programs while multitasking. There's no option for a discrete GPU, but that's to be expected with ThinkPads, and the laptop's ultra-slim design doesn't really allow for it. However, the Core Ultra GPU's integrated Intel Arc graphics are quite good.

2:48
Related
Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon (Gen 12) review: More power and improved features in a smaller body

Lenovo continues to find ways to improve on a what is already the best business laptop on the market.

The 14-inch 1920 x 1200 display has a 16:10 aspect ratio, giving you enough screen real estate to work comfortably, and there are touchscreen and 2.8K upgrades available (although no 4K option, sadly). Admittedly, the X1 Carbon Gen 12 isn't for everyone. Not all users may appreciate the style and quirks of the ThinkPad design, such as the TrackPoint or double trackpad buttons. The RAM is also soldered, meaning that it can't be upgraded and may not be a great choice for tinkerers and DIYers. Nevertheless, the ThinkPad X1 Carbon is the best 14-inch machine for professionals who need a no-nonsense workhorse laptop.

Best workstation
HP ZBook Firefly 14 G10

For when you need the most power

The HP ZBook Firefly G10 is a beautiful and powerful 14-inch laptop with enough power and ports to replace a desktop workstation. It packs powerful hardware and offers a ton of customization options, making it relatively easy (although not necessarily cheap) to build the perfect mobile workstation for your needs.

Pros
  • Excellent hardware for productivity
  • Many configuration options
  • Spacious keyboard with number pad
  • Sleek and attractive design
Cons
  • It's costly
  • Better CPU choices for the price
$1877 at HP $1299 at Amazon

You can use just about any laptop to do basic work, but a good workstation laptop offers enough power and features to replace your desktop PC entirely. They're built for running resource-heavy programs such as specialized professional software, and often feature discrete GPUs and advanced display technologies for creative work. The HP ZBook 14 G10 is our favorite 14-inch laptop workstation for this purpose, packing an excellent suite of hardware (with tons of upgrade options) into a sleek and lightweight frame.

The HP ZBook 14 G10 mobile workstation runs on 13th-gen Intel Core CPUs, and if you build and order your machine through HP's website, you will have a myriad of customization options to choose from. CPU options include Core i5 and i7 U-Series processors. For graphics, you can opt for the standard Iris Xe integrated GPU or an Nvidia RTX A500 GPU. Memory options range from 8GB to 64GB of speedy 5200MHz DDR5 RAM. Whatever your needs and budget may be, chances are you can build a ZBook 14 G10 to suit.

Typically, we'd like to see more powerful CPU and GPU options, such as HX-series Intel processors or the GeForce RTX 40-series graphics cards. The entry-level 14-inch 16:10 display could also be brighter (although the upgraded panels are excellent for design professionals, provided you're willing to pay for them). Overall, however, the HP ZBook 14 G10 is a highly capable workstation for mobile professionals.

Best convertible
Lenovo Yoga 9i (2024)

A tablet and laptop in one

8.5 / 10
$1100 $1450 Save $350

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is a capable and affordable 2-in-1 convertible. It offers good performance and impressive battery life for such a sleek frame, and its 14-inch fold-flat touch display features a 3.2K resolution. It's a versatile laptop for students, professionals, and anybody else looking for a good 14-inch convertible.

Pros
  • Good performance and battery life
  • Versatile 2-in-1 design
  • Impressive 3.2K touch display
Cons
  • A bit expensive
  • Limited configuration options
$1100 at Best Buy $1100 at Lenovo

Convertible laptops are no longer a novelty, with an increasing number of people adopting these versatile 2-in-1 touchscreen computers. Convertibles feature touch displays that fold all the way backward so they can lay totally flat, allowing you to use the laptop much like a tablet. You don't have to pay much to get a good 2-in-1 today, either, with the 2024 Lenovo Yoga 9i being one of our favorite new convertible laptops thanks to its good performance, sharp display, and very attractive price.

Like many of our other picks, the Yoga 9i features an Intel Core UItra and a 14-inch 16:10 touch display. The OLED screen is superb, too, featuring a 3.2K resolution and 100% DCI-P3 color accuracy. The touch display supports the Lenovo Slim Pen, which is included with the laptop.

 Related
Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i (2024): Everything you need to know

The Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i is one of Lenovo's best laptops for creativity, featuring Intel Core Ultra CPUs and plenty of AI features

The build quality is quite good, as you'd expect from Lenovo. The frame is mostly aluminum, which adds to durability but also its weight. At nearly three pounds, the Lenovo Yoga 7i isn't a featherweight 2-in-1 by any means. Its smart design extends to the port layout, too, which includes two Thunderbolt 4 connections with support for external displays. The built-in 5MP 2.5K webcam is also great for professionals and students who may need a quality camera for video meetings and online classes.

For Mac users
MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)

An Apple a day...

$1299 $1599 Save $300

The MacBook Pro was already one of the best laptops out there, and the new M3 chip makes it even better. Its impressive CPU is not only powerful, but offers incredible battery life thanks to its 3nm architecture. The mini-LED Retina display is also gorgeous. It's pricey, but if you're looking for the best Windows laptop alternative, the MacBook Pro M3 is it.

Pros
  • Powerful and efficient
  • Gorgeous Retina display
  • Great-sounding six-speaker audio
  • Superb battery life
Cons
  • Expensive, even without upgrades
  • Distracting display notch
$1299 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3) $1299 at Amazon (14-inch, M3)

If you don't use Windows, then chances are you're a Mac person (not to slight the ChromeOS and Linux people out there). The MacBook Pro has always been the cream of the macOS laptop crop, and although the last-gen M2 chipset is hardly long in the tooth in 2024, Apple updated the Pro lineup again with the newer M3 hardware. The MacBook Pro M3 is faster and more efficient than ever before, making it far and away the best 14-inch laptop for anybody in the market for a Windows alternative.

One noteworthy change from the last-gen MacBook Pro M2 is that the newer model is available with the base M3 chip, whereas previously, the MacBook Pro was only offered with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips. This gives MacBook users a third, cheaper option than Pro or Max. However, if you're spending "MacBook Pro money," we recommend sticking with the M3 Pro or M3 Max models, especially for creative work. The new M3 Pro and M3 Max are seriously impressive, combining many CPU and GPU cores onto a single chip that delivers blazing-fast performance. It does it efficiently, too, with its 3nm architecture delivering fantastic battery life despite its power and functionality.

 Related
Which MacBook Pro (M3, 2023) should I get?

Apple's new laptops are packed with features, but which is the best for you?

A MacBook Pro is more than just its internal hardware, though, and the exterior of this laptop doesn't disappoint. It features a sleek design with great build quality, which is no surprise for a MacBook. The display is particularly eye-catching, with a gorgeous mini-LED panel with a peak brightness of 1,600 nits. Some users may not appreciate the notch at the top, but we can live with it for a display that looks this lovely. Six speakers also offer surprisingly great sound for a laptop, making this laptop a true multimedia machine. It's expensive, especially once you factor in upgrades, but the MacBook Pro M3 is a truly excellent computer for creators, professionals, and die-hard Mac fans.

Best Chromebook
Acer Chromebook Spin 714 (2023)

A great, advanced Chromebook

$530 $578 Save $48

Most Chromebooks feature Spartan hardware and mediocre displays, but the Acer Chromebook Spin 714 looks and performs more like a standard mid-range laptop. That's owed to its modern hardware, which includes 13th-gen Core CPUs, as well as its nice 14-inch 16:10 touchscreen. It also feels sturdy, thanks to its partial aluminum construction.

Pros
  • 13th-generation Intel Core CPU
  • Premium look and feel
  • Durable aluminum construction
  • Versatile convertible form factor
Cons
  • ChromeOS isn't for everyone
  • Somewhat pricey for a Chromebook
$530 at Amazon $530 at Best Buy

Chromebooks are becoming more common, but many people have been slow to accept these Chrome OS laptops due to their perceived inferiority to Windows laptops and MacBooks. That's understandable, as Chromebooks often feature rather basic hardware and mediocre displays. The Acer Chromebook Spin 714, by contrast, offers the look, feel, and performance of a "regular" laptop, combining modern PC hardware with a nice overall design.

The Acer Chromebook Spin 714 is actually one of very few Chrome OS laptops sporting 13th-generation Intel Core CPUs, putting it in the same performance class as most of our other picks. It packs a 10-core i5-1335U processor and 8GB of RAM. That's plenty of juice for basic workloads and everyday tasks like browsing the web and streaming. You also get a proper SSD, with between 256GB and 2TB of storage depending on your needs.

The Chromebook Spin 714 is a 2-in-1, so a good display is important. Thankfully, it doesn't disappoint. The 14-inch fold-flat touchscreen has a resolution of 1920x1200 with a 16:10 aspect ratio. It doesn't come with a stylus, unfortunately, which we would have liked. However, the laptop looks and feels great, featuring partially aluminum construction that makes the Spin 714 2-in-1 feel more substantial than most Chromebooks. It's a bit pricier than those other Chromebooks though, of course. But considering how much you get for your money, the Spin 714 is the best 14-inch laptop for Chrome OS users at a solid value.

Best 14-inch laptops in 2024: Bottom line

Laptops with 14-inch displays are becoming increasingly popular, and you have many more options to choose from today than you did a few years ago. For us, the 2024 Dell XPS 14 ticks the most boxes for what we want to see in a laptop of this size. It sports a stunning OLED display, powerful hardware, and a slick design that includes a comfortable keyboard and large glass touchpad. If you don't need a 2-in-1 or a gaming laptop, the Dell XPS 14 is a fantastic machine for work and everyday use.

Best overall
Dell XPS 14
9 / 10
$1500 $2100 Save $600

The Dell XPS 14 is a highly-capable laptop with a 14-inch screen and offers the choice of some powerful CPU and GPU specs.

$1500 at Dell $1500 at Best Buy

However, the Dell XPS 14 is a premium laptop with a price tag to match. If you're on a budget or just want something simple, consider one of our other picks. The Acer Aspire 3 is an affordable workhorse laptop that offers the basics for a very reasonable price. The Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 is another great value in a sea of gaming laptops that regularly retail for north of $2,000. It offers solid performance and gaming versatility in a professional-looking package. The bottom line is that there are a lot of great 14-inch laptops out there, so you can definitely find something you'll like.

$450 at Amazon

It even comes with a protective sleeve.

Best gaming laptop
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (Gen 8)

A great gaming machine for a low(er) price

Lenovo's Legion lineup features some very capable (and often surprisingly affordable) gaming machines. The 8th-generation Legion Slim 5 14 packs impressive performance inside a reasonably compact 14-inch frame. It's available with an AMD Ryzen 7 CPU and Nvidia RTX 40-series graphics, so you've got plenty of muscle for modern gaming.

Pros
  • Powerful for its size and price
  • Outstanding 2.8K OLED screen
  • 16:10 display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Unassuming design
Cons
  • Limited configuration options
  • No G-Sync or Thunderbolt support
$1450 at Lenovo

Gaming laptops have come a long way in recent years, and while most sport larger 15- to 17-inch displays, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 shows that you can get it done in a smaller package, too. This unassuming gaming laptop features a 14-inch 16:10 display, giving gamers a more compact and portable alternative to gaming laptops (some of which are rather large and cumbersome).

Notably, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 is configured with an AMD Ryzen 7 CPU and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 mobile GPU. Nvidia has the lion's share of the gaming laptop market, so that's no surprise, but it's got plenty of horsepower for a laptop at this price. Better still, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 comes standard with an outstanding 2.8K OLED display, which is usually the sort of upgrade you have to pay a premium for. That said, there's no G-Sync support.

 Related
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (2023) review: The 14-inch gaming laptop to beat

A balance of price, features, and performance in a thin and light laptop.

Still, with that RTX 4060 GPU and 16GB of 6400MHz DDR5 RAM, you won't be disappointed with the performance, even if the memory is soldered. This gaming laptop is more than capable of running newer games well, and they'll look great on the HDR 500 display, which features a 120Hz refresh rate for smooth sailing. Although pickings are slim in the 14-inch gaming laptop niche specifically, the Lenovo Legion 5 Slim 14 doesn't feel like it makes many compromises with its smaller size.

Best for creators
Surface Laptop Studio 2

With a unique form factor, too

$1950 $2400 Save $450

The Microsoft Surface Laptop Studio 2 is a unique take on the traditional 2-in-1 convertible laptop design. Its responsive 14.4-inch touchscreen can be positioned in several ways or even folded totally flat, letting you use the Laptop Studio 2 as a tablet. This convertible also packs very impressive hardware, including the option for a discrete Nvidia RTX GPU.

Pros
  • Incredibly versatile design
  • Great hardware
  • Discrete graphics
  • Responsive 14.4-inch touchscreen
Cons
  • Quite expensive
  • Heavy, especially for a 2-in-1
$2000 at Microsoft $2400 at Best Buy $1950 at Amazon

Microsoft's Surface Laptop series spans a huge range of features and prices, and the Studio Laptop 2 is the company's most powerful (and most expensive) Surface machine yet. This unique convertible puts a fresh spin on the standard 2-in-1 design, featuring a bright and color-accurate 14.4-inch touch display that can be pulled forward towards you instead of just flipped up. The stylus pen included with the Laptop Studio 2 now supports haptic feedback, making writing and drawing much more responsive than it was on the previous model.

The Surface Laptop Studio 2 packs impressive hardware under the hood, too. It comes standard with a 13th-generation Intel Core i7 CPU and 16GB of RAM (upgradable to 64GB). The base configuration features Intel Iris Xe integrated graphics, but it's available with an Nvidia RTX discrete GPU, which is an upgrade we'd recommend for creative professionals such as video editors and graphic designers. Combined with its excellent touch display and haptic stylus pen, a dedicated graphics card makes the Surface Laptop Studio 2 a creator's dream.

 Related
Microsoft Surface Laptop Studio 2 review: The most powerful Surface ever

The Surface Laptop Studio 2 is all about more power

Of course, as Microsoft's most powerful and feature-rich Surface device, the Laptop Studio 2 is not cheap. Pricing starts at $2,000 for the base configuration without a discrete GPU. This convertible is also rather heavy, weighing a little more than four pounds. Still, if you're looking for the best 14-inch laptop for creative work, the Surface Laptop Studio 2 is a powerful and versatile machine that can handle almost anything you'd need a computer to do.

Best business laptop
Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12

And with that iconic ThinkPad design

$1820 $2030 Save $210

The ThinkPad name has been synonymous with "business laptops" for decades now. The 12th-generation ThinkPad X1 Carbon lives up to that pedigree with its modern hardware, solid build quality, sleek and lightweight design, and iconic ThinkPad styling.

Pros
  • Premium Core Ultra performance
  • Sleek design with classic ThinkPad styling
  • 1080p Full HD webcam
  • Slim and lightweight
Cons
  • ThinkPad design is not for everyone
  • Soldered RAM
$1362 at Lenovo $1820 at Best Buy $1230 at Newegg

If you're at all familiar with business laptops, then the ThinkPad series needs no introduction. ThinkPad laptops have been a staple in offices around the world for decades, and the X1 Carbon line has helped Lenovo maintain the ThinkPad pedigree in the age of ultrabooks and 2-in-1s. The newest 12th-generation ThinkPad X1 Carbon is our pick for the best 14-inch laptop for business professionals thanks to its excellent hardware, powerful performance, and great design wrapped in the iconic styling that made the ThinkPads famous.

The ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12 comes loaded with Intel's latest Core Ultra CPUs and comes standard with 16GB of RAM (which you can upgrade to 64GB, although it's soldered), so performance is no issue. The ThinkPad X1 Carbon is a proven performer when it comes to handling heavy workloads, running demanding productivity software, and juggling programs while multitasking. There's no option for a discrete GPU, but that's to be expected with ThinkPads, and the laptop's ultra-slim design doesn't really allow for it. However, the Core Ultra GPU's integrated Intel Arc graphics are quite good.

2:48
Related
Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon (Gen 12) review: More power and improved features in a smaller body

Lenovo continues to find ways to improve on a what is already the best business laptop on the market.

The 14-inch 1920 x 1200 display has a 16:10 aspect ratio, giving you enough screen real estate to work comfortably, and there are touchscreen and 2.8K upgrades available (although no 4K option, sadly). Admittedly, the X1 Carbon Gen 12 isn't for everyone. Not all users may appreciate the style and quirks of the ThinkPad design, such as the TrackPoint or double trackpad buttons. The RAM is also soldered, meaning that it can't be upgraded and may not be a great choice for tinkerers and DIYers. Nevertheless, the ThinkPad X1 Carbon is the best 14-inch machine for professionals who need a no-nonsense workhorse laptop.

Best workstation
HP ZBook Firefly 14 G10

For when you need the most power

The HP ZBook Firefly G10 is a beautiful and powerful 14-inch laptop with enough power and ports to replace a desktop workstation. It packs powerful hardware and offers a ton of customization options, making it relatively easy (although not necessarily cheap) to build the perfect mobile workstation for your needs.

Pros
  • Excellent hardware for productivity
  • Many configuration options
  • Spacious keyboard with number pad
  • Sleek and attractive design
Cons
  • It's costly
  • Better CPU choices for the price
$1877 at HP $1299 at Amazon

You can use just about any laptop to do basic work, but a good workstation laptop offers enough power and features to replace your desktop PC entirely. They're built for running resource-heavy programs such as specialized professional software, and often feature discrete GPUs and advanced display technologies for creative work. The HP ZBook 14 G10 is our favorite 14-inch laptop workstation for this purpose, packing an excellent suite of hardware (with tons of upgrade options) into a sleek and lightweight frame.

The HP ZBook 14 G10 mobile workstation runs on 13th-gen Intel Core CPUs, and if you build and order your machine through HP's website, you will have a myriad of customization options to choose from. CPU options include Core i5 and i7 U-Series processors. For graphics, you can opt for the standard Iris Xe integrated GPU or an Nvidia RTX A500 GPU. Memory options range from 8GB to 64GB of speedy 5200MHz DDR5 RAM. Whatever your needs and budget may be, chances are you can build a ZBook 14 G10 to suit.

Typically, we'd like to see more powerful CPU and GPU options, such as HX-series Intel processors or the GeForce RTX 40-series graphics cards. The entry-level 14-inch 16:10 display could also be brighter (although the upgraded panels are excellent for design professionals, provided you're willing to pay for them). Overall, however, the HP ZBook 14 G10 is a highly capable workstation for mobile professionals.

Best convertible
Lenovo Yoga 9i (2024)

A tablet and laptop in one

8.5 / 10
$1100 $1450 Save $350

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is a capable and affordable 2-in-1 convertible. It offers good performance and impressive battery life for such a sleek frame, and its 14-inch fold-flat touch display features a 3.2K resolution. It's a versatile laptop for students, professionals, and anybody else looking for a good 14-inch convertible.

Pros
  • Good performance and battery life
  • Versatile 2-in-1 design
  • Impressive 3.2K touch display
Cons
  • A bit expensive
  • Limited configuration options
$1100 at Best Buy $1100 at Lenovo

Convertible laptops are no longer a novelty, with an increasing number of people adopting these versatile 2-in-1 touchscreen computers. Convertibles feature touch displays that fold all the way backward so they can lay totally flat, allowing you to use the laptop much like a tablet. You don't have to pay much to get a good 2-in-1 today, either, with the 2024 Lenovo Yoga 9i being one of our favorite new convertible laptops thanks to its good performance, sharp display, and very attractive price.

Like many of our other picks, the Yoga 9i features an Intel Core UItra and a 14-inch 16:10 touch display. The OLED screen is superb, too, featuring a 3.2K resolution and 100% DCI-P3 color accuracy. The touch display supports the Lenovo Slim Pen, which is included with the laptop.

 Related
Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i (2024): Everything you need to know

The Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i is one of Lenovo's best laptops for creativity, featuring Intel Core Ultra CPUs and plenty of AI features

The build quality is quite good, as you'd expect from Lenovo. The frame is mostly aluminum, which adds to durability but also its weight. At nearly three pounds, the Lenovo Yoga 7i isn't a featherweight 2-in-1 by any means. Its smart design extends to the port layout, too, which includes two Thunderbolt 4 connections with support for external displays. The built-in 5MP 2.5K webcam is also great for professionals and students who may need a quality camera for video meetings and online classes.

For Mac users
MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)

An Apple a day...

$1299 $1599 Save $300

The MacBook Pro was already one of the best laptops out there, and the new M3 chip makes it even better. Its impressive CPU is not only powerful, but offers incredible battery life thanks to its 3nm architecture. The mini-LED Retina display is also gorgeous. It's pricey, but if you're looking for the best Windows laptop alternative, the MacBook Pro M3 is it.

Pros
  • Powerful and efficient
  • Gorgeous Retina display
  • Great-sounding six-speaker audio
  • Superb battery life
Cons
  • Expensive, even without upgrades
  • Distracting display notch
$1299 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3) $1299 at Amazon (14-inch, M3)

If you don't use Windows, then chances are you're a Mac person (not to slight the ChromeOS and Linux people out there). The MacBook Pro has always been the cream of the macOS laptop crop, and although the last-gen M2 chipset is hardly long in the tooth in 2024, Apple updated the Pro lineup again with the newer M3 hardware. The MacBook Pro M3 is faster and more efficient than ever before, making it far and away the best 14-inch laptop for anybody in the market for a Windows alternative.

One noteworthy change from the last-gen MacBook Pro M2 is that the newer model is available with the base M3 chip, whereas previously, the MacBook Pro was only offered with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips. This gives MacBook users a third, cheaper option than Pro or Max. However, if you're spending "MacBook Pro money," we recommend sticking with the M3 Pro or M3 Max models, especially for creative work. The new M3 Pro and M3 Max are seriously impressive, combining many CPU and GPU cores onto a single chip that delivers blazing-fast performance. It does it efficiently, too, with its 3nm architecture delivering fantastic battery life despite its power and functionality.

 Related
Which MacBook Pro (M3, 2023) should I get?

Apple's new laptops are packed with features, but which is the best for you?

A MacBook Pro is more than just its internal hardware, though, and the exterior of this laptop doesn't disappoint. It features a sleek design with great build quality, which is no surprise for a MacBook. The display is particularly eye-catching, with a gorgeous mini-LED panel with a peak brightness of 1,600 nits. Some users may not appreciate the notch at the top, but we can live with it for a display that looks this lovely. Six speakers also offer surprisingly great sound for a laptop, making this laptop a true multimedia machine. It's expensive, especially once you factor in upgrades, but the MacBook Pro M3 is a truly excellent computer for creators, professionals, and die-hard Mac fans.

Best Chromebook
Acer Chromebook Spin 714 (2023)

A great, advanced Chromebook

$530 $578 Save $48

Most Chromebooks feature Spartan hardware and mediocre displays, but the Acer Chromebook Spin 714 looks and performs more like a standard mid-range laptop. That's owed to its modern hardware, which includes 13th-gen Core CPUs, as well as its nice 14-inch 16:10 touchscreen. It also feels sturdy, thanks to its partial aluminum construction.

Pros
  • 13th-generation Intel Core CPU
  • Premium look and feel
  • Durable aluminum construction
  • Versatile convertible form factor
Cons
  • ChromeOS isn't for everyone
  • Somewhat pricey for a Chromebook
$530 at Amazon $530 at Best Buy

Chromebooks are becoming more common, but many people have been slow to accept these Chrome OS laptops due to their perceived inferiority to Windows laptops and MacBooks. That's understandable, as Chromebooks often feature rather basic hardware and mediocre displays. The Acer Chromebook Spin 714, by contrast, offers the look, feel, and performance of a "regular" laptop, combining modern PC hardware with a nice overall design.

The Acer Chromebook Spin 714 is actually one of very few Chrome OS laptops sporting 13th-generation Intel Core CPUs, putting it in the same performance class as most of our other picks. It packs a 10-core i5-1335U processor and 8GB of RAM. That's plenty of juice for basic workloads and everyday tasks like browsing the web and streaming. You also get a proper SSD, with between 256GB and 2TB of storage depending on your needs.

The Chromebook Spin 714 is a 2-in-1, so a good display is important. Thankfully, it doesn't disappoint. The 14-inch fold-flat touchscreen has a resolution of 1920x1200 with a 16:10 aspect ratio. It doesn't come with a stylus, unfortunately, which we would have liked. However, the laptop looks and feels great, featuring partially aluminum construction that makes the Spin 714 2-in-1 feel more substantial than most Chromebooks. It's a bit pricier than those other Chromebooks though, of course. But considering how much you get for your money, the Spin 714 is the best 14-inch laptop for Chrome OS users at a solid value.

Best 14-inch laptops in 2024: Bottom line

Laptops with 14-inch displays are becoming increasingly popular, and you have many more options to choose from today than you did a few years ago. For us, the 2024 Dell XPS 14 ticks the most boxes for what we want to see in a laptop of this size. It sports a stunning OLED display, powerful hardware, and a slick design that includes a comfortable keyboard and large glass touchpad. If you don't need a 2-in-1 or a gaming laptop, the Dell XPS 14 is a fantastic machine for work and everyday use.

Best overall
Dell XPS 14
9 / 10
$1500 $2100 Save $600

The Dell XPS 14 is a highly-capable laptop with a 14-inch screen and offers the choice of some powerful CPU and GPU specs.

$1500 at Dell $1500 at Best Buy

However, the Dell XPS 14 is a premium laptop with a price tag to match. If you're on a budget or just want something simple, consider one of our other picks. The Acer Aspire 3 is an affordable workhorse laptop that offers the basics for a very reasonable price. The Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 is another great value in a sea of gaming laptops that regularly retail for north of $2,000. It offers solid performance and gaming versatility in a professional-looking package. The bottom line is that there are a lot of great 14-inch laptops out there, so you can definitely find something you'll like.
item.content:encoded:
There's a wide variety of laptop sizes today, with most popular models landing somewhere in the 13-inch to 16-inch range. Of these, 14-inch laptops arguably hit the sweet spot in size, offering the perfect balance of utility and portability. The best laptops at this size are small and light enough to carry around, but 14-inch screens are still comfortable enough for productive work. Some 14-inch laptop keyboards even accommodate a number pad, which is not something you're likely to find on smaller machines. This makes 14-inch laptops an excellent choice for students, professionals, and gamers on the go. Since the market has grown significantly in recent years, you have plenty of options to choose from. Whether you want a MacBook, a 2-in-1 convertible, a gaming laptop, or an affordable Chrome OS workhorse, we've rounded up all the best 14-inch laptops right here.

    Dell XPS 14
    Best overall
    $1500 at Dell
  • Source: Amazon
    Acer Aspire 3 A314 Slim Laptop
    Best value
    $450 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (Gen 8)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1450 at Lenovo
  • Surface Laptop Studio 2
    Best for creators
    $1950 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12
    Best business laptop
    $1230 at Newegg
  • Source: HP
    HP ZBook Firefly 14 G10
    Best workstation
    $1299 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo Yoga 9i (2024)
    Best convertible
    $1100 at Best Buy
  • MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)
    For Mac users
    $1299 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3)
  • Acer Chromebook Spin 714 (2023)
    Best Chromebook
    $530 at Amazon

Find your next "sweet spot" sized laptop among these 14-inch options

Best overall
Dell XPS 14

One of the best options in 2024, period

9 / 10
$1500 $2100 Save $600

Dell's XPS 14 is not just one of the best 14-inch laptops, it's one of the best laptops of 2024, period. It offers up to a stunning 3.2K OLED display with a smooth 120Hz refresh rate, and Intel's latest Core Ultra processors deliver great performance for work and multitasking. There's even a discrete GPU option.

Pros
  • 3.2K 120Hz OLED display option is gorgeous
  • Great design and build quality
  • Snappy Intel Core Ultra processor
  • Optional Nvidia RTX GPU
Cons
  • Fairly heavy for its size
  • No legacy USB-A ports
$1500 at Dell $1500 at Best Buy

Dell has been at the top of the Windows laptop market in recent years thanks to its excellent XPS line. The Dell XPS 14 2024 is one of our favorite recent releases and our top pick overall among the best 14-inch laptops. With its aluminum chassis, the XPS 14 looks and feels like a premium laptop, even if its metal construction makes it noticeably heavier than other machines in this category.

That premium design extends to the 14-inch screen. It comes standard with a 1200p FHD+ panel, but we favor the gorgeous 3.2K OLED upgrade with a buttery smooth variable refresh rate of 120Hz. It also has an aspect ratio of 16:10, something that's thankfully becoming more commonplace on laptops and gives you a taller display area to work in. The Dell XPS 14 runs on Intel's latest Core Ultra 7 155H processor and has 16GB of memory in its base configuration (which you can upgrade to up to 64GB), ensuring great overall performance for work, entertainment, and multitasking. There's also an optional GeForce RTX 4050 discrete GPU upgrade, allowing you to game on this thing if you're so inclined.

Related
Dell XPS 14 (2024) review: It's just so much fun

A MacBook Pro killer

Also, a 512GB solid-state drive comes standard, which should provide ample storage for most people, but you can upgrade to as much as 4TB of storage if you need to. Ports include HDMI, USB Type-C, a microSD card reader, and two Thunderbolt 4 connections, so you've got plenty of options for hooking up peripherals such as an external display, although it lacks any USB-A ports. It's a little pricey, but if you're looking for the best all-around 14-inch laptop on the market today, the Dell XPS 14 is the one to buy.

Best value
Acer Aspire 3 A314 Slim Laptop

When all you need are the basics

The Acer Aspire 3 A314 is a solid, no-frills 14-inch laptop for shoppers on a budget. Despite its affordable price, it features a nice full HD display and solid hardware. And, while the design is a bit basic, it doesn't look or feel cheap.

Pros
  • Full HD IPS display
  • Solid specs for the price
  • Good port setup
  • Includes a sleeve
Cons
  • Build quality is nothing special
  • No numpad
$450 at Amazon

Acer is no stranger to the budget laptop market and makes a lot of solid Windows PCs and Chromebooks for users who don't want to spend a lot of money. If you just need a basic but reliable 14-inch laptop, the Acer Aspire 3 A314 fits the bill. This model is available in various configurations, all running on AMD Ryzen CPUs, but the one that hits the sweet spot for us is the Ryzen 5 7520U model with 8GB of LDDR5 RAM. On top of that, you've got a 512GB SSD, which is a generous amount of storage to see at this price.

Although it's a budget laptop, the Acer Aspire 3 A314 doesn't look or feel cheap. It has a sleek and attractive design and offers a good selection of ports, including an HDMI, a USB Type-C, and two USB Type-A ports. It also features Wi-Fi 6 wireless connectivity. It's not the thinnest laptop among our picks, but it's suitably thin at just under three-quarters of an inch. The keyboard is also nicely sized, although it lacks a number pad. Some users may prefer this for a less crowded key layout though.

The 14-inch display is a 1080p IPS panel, which is another good thing to see in this price bracket (even if the bezels are a bit chunky). Many cheaper laptops cut corners with pared-down mobile hardware and 720p screens, but the Acer Aspire 3 A314 shows that you don't have to sacrifice too much to get an affordable machine built for daily use. It even comes with a protective sleeve.

Best gaming laptop
Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (Gen 8)

A great gaming machine for a low(er) price

Lenovo's Legion lineup features some very capable (and often surprisingly affordable) gaming machines. The 8th-generation Legion Slim 5 14 packs impressive performance inside a reasonably compact 14-inch frame. It's available with an AMD Ryzen 7 CPU and Nvidia RTX 40-series graphics, so you've got plenty of muscle for modern gaming.

Pros
  • Powerful for its size and price
  • Outstanding 2.8K OLED screen
  • 16:10 display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Unassuming design
Cons
  • Limited configuration options
  • No G-Sync or Thunderbolt support
$1450 at Lenovo

Gaming laptops have come a long way in recent years, and while most sport larger 15- to 17-inch displays, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 shows that you can get it done in a smaller package, too. This unassuming gaming laptop features a 14-inch 16:10 display, giving gamers a more compact and portable alternative to gaming laptops (some of which are rather large and cumbersome).

Notably, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 is configured with an AMD Ryzen 7 CPU and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 mobile GPU. Nvidia has the lion's share of the gaming laptop market, so that's no surprise, but it's got plenty of horsepower for a laptop at this price. Better still, the Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 comes standard with an outstanding 2.8K OLED display, which is usually the sort of upgrade you have to pay a premium for. That said, there's no G-Sync support.

Lenovo Legion Slim 5 14 (2023) review: The 14-inch gaming laptop to beat

A balance of price, features, and performance in a thin and light laptop.

Still, with that RTX 4060 GPU and 16GB of 6400MHz DDR5 RAM, you won't be disappointed with the performance, even if the memory is soldered. This gaming laptop is more than capable of running newer games well, and they'll look great on the HDR 500 display, which features a 120Hz refresh rate for smooth sailing. Although pickings are slim in the 14-inch gaming laptop niche specifically, the Lenovo Legion 5 Slim 14 doesn't feel like it makes many compromises with its smaller size.

Best for creators
Surface Laptop Studio 2

With a unique form factor, too

$1950 $2400 Save $450

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 12

CPU options include Core i5 and i7 U-Series processors. For graphics, you can opt for the standard Iris Xe integrated GPU or an Nvidia RTX A500 GPU. Memory options range from 8GB to 64GB of speedy 5200MHz DDR5 RAM. Whatever your needs and budget may be, chances are you can build a ZBook 14 G10 to suit.

Typically, we'd like to see more powerful CPU and GPU options, such as HX-series Intel processors or the GeForce RTX 40-series graphics cards. The entry-level 14-inch 16:10 display could also be brighter (although the upgraded panels are excellent for design professionals, provided you're willing to pay for them). Overall, however, the HP ZBook 14 G10 is a highly capable workstation for mobile professionals.

Best convertible
Lenovo Yoga 9i (2024)

A tablet and laptop in one

8.5 / 10
$1100 $1450 Save $350

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is a capable and affordable 2-in-1 convertible. It offers good performance and impressive battery life for such a sleek frame, and its 14-inch fold-flat touch display features a 3.2K resolution. It's a versatile laptop for students, professionals, and anybody else looking for a good 14-inch convertible.

Convertible laptops are no longer a novelty, with an increasing number of people adopting these versatile 2-in-1 touchscreen computers. Convertibles feature touch displays that fold all the way backward so they can lay totally flat, allowing you to use the laptop much like a tablet. You don't have to pay much to get a good 2-in-1 today, either, with the 2024 Lenovo Yoga 9i being one of our favorite new convertible laptops thanks to its good performance, sharp display, and very attractive price.

Like many of our other picks, the Yoga 9i features an Intel Core UItra and a 14-inch 16:10 touch display. The OLED screen is superb, too, featuring a 3.2K resolution and 100% DCI-P3 color accuracy. The touch display supports the Lenovo Slim Pen, which is included with the laptop.

Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i (2024): Everything you need to know

The Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i is one of Lenovo's best laptops for creativity, featuring Intel Core Ultra CPUs and plenty of AI features

The build quality is quite good, as you'd expect from Lenovo. The frame is mostly aluminum, which adds to durability but also its weight. At nearly three pounds, the Lenovo Yoga 7i isn't a featherweight 2-in-1 by any means. Its smart design extends to the port layout, too, which includes two Thunderbolt 4 connections with support for external displays. The built-in 5MP 2.5K webcam is also great for professionals and students who may need a quality camera for video meetings and online classes.

For Mac users
MacBook Pro (M3, 2023)

An Apple a day...

$1299 $1599 Save $300

The MacBook Pro was already one of the best laptops out there, and the new M3 chip makes it even better. Its impressive CPU is not only powerful, but offers incredible battery life thanks to its 3nm architecture. The mini-LED Retina display is also gorgeous. It's pricey, but if you're looking for the best Windows laptop alternative, the MacBook Pro M3 is it.

Pros
  • Powerful and efficient
  • Gorgeous Retina display
  • Great-sounding six-speaker audio
  • Superb battery life
Cons
  • Expensive, even without upgrades
  • Distracting display notch
$1299 at Best Buy (14-inch, M3) $1299 at Amazon (14-inch, M3)

If you don't use Windows, then chances are you're a Mac person (not to slight the ChromeOS and Linux people out there). The MacBook Pro has always been the cream of the macOS laptop crop, and although the last-gen M2 chipset is hardly long in the tooth in 2024, Apple updated the Pro lineup again with the newer M3 hardware. The MacBook Pro M3 is faster and more efficient than ever before, making it far and away the best 14-inch laptop for anybody in the market for a Windows alternative.

One noteworthy change from the last-gen MacBook Pro M2 is that the newer model is available with the base M3 chip, whereas previously, the MacBook Pro was only offered with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips. This gives MacBook users a third, cheaper option than Pro or Max. However, if you're spending "MacBook Pro money," we recommend sticking with the M3 Pro or M3 Max models, especially for creative work. The new M3 Pro and M3 Max are seriously impressive, combining many CPU and GPU cores onto a single chip that delivers blazing-fast performance. It does it efficiently, too, with its 3nm architecture delivering fantastic battery life despite its power and functionality.

 Related
Which MacBook Pro (M3, 2023) should I get?

Apple's new laptops are packed with features, but which is the best for you?

A MacBook Pro is more than just its internal hardware, though, and the exterior of this laptop doesn't disappoint. It features a sleek design with great build quality, which is no surprise for a MacBook. The display is particularly eye-catching, with a gorgeous mini-LED panel with a peak brightness of 1,600 nits. Some users may not appreciate the notch at the top, but we can live with it for a display that looks this lovely. Six speakers also offer surprisingly great sound for a laptop, making this laptop a true multimedia machine. It's expensive, especially once you factor in upgrades, but the MacBook Pro M3 is a truly excellent computer for creators, professionals, and die-hard Mac fans.

Best Chromebook
Acer Chromebook Spin 714 (2023)

A great, advanced Chromebook

$530 $578 Save $48

Most Chromebooks feature Spartan hardware and mediocre displays, but the Acer Chromebook Spin 714 looks and performs more like a standard mid-range laptop. That's owed to its modern hardware, which includes 13th-gen Core CPUs, as well as its nice 14-inch 16:10 touchscreen. It also feels sturdy, thanks to its partial aluminum construction.

Pros
  • 13th-generation Intel Core CPU
  • Premium look and feel
  • Durable aluminum construction
  • Versatile convertible form factor
Cons
  • ChromeOS isn't for everyone

$530 at Amazon $530 at Best Buy

Chromebooks are becoming more common, but many people have been slow to accept these Chrome OS laptops due to their perceived inferiority to Windows laptops and MacBooks. That's understandable, as Chromebooks often feature rather basic hardware and mediocre displays. The Acer Chromebook Spin 714, by contrast, offers the look, feel, and performance of a "regular" laptop, combining modern PC hardware with a nice overall design.

The Acer Chromebook Spin 714 is actually one of very few Chrome OS laptops sporting 13th-generation Intel Core CPUs, putting it in the same performance class as most of our other picks. It packs a 10-core i5-1335U processor and 8GB of RAM. That's plenty of juice for basic workloads and everyday tasks like browsing the web and streaming. You also get a proper SSD, with between 256GB and 2TB of storage depending on your needs.

The Chromebook Spin 714 is a 2-in-1, so a good display is important. Thankfully, it doesn't disappoint. The 14-inch fold-flat touchscreen has a resolution of 1920x1200 with a 16:10 aspect ratio. It doesn't come with a stylus, unfortunately, which we would have liked. However, the laptop looks and feels great, featuring partially aluminum construction that makes the Spin 714 2-in-1 feel more substantial than most Chromebooks. It's a bit pricier than those other Chromebooks though, of course. But considering how much you get for your money, the Spin 714 is the best 14-inch laptop for Chrome OS users at a solid value.

Best 14-inch laptops in 2024: Bottom line

Laptops with 14-inch displays are becoming increasingly popular, and you have many more options to choose from today than you did a few years ago. For us, the 2024 Dell XPS 14 ticks the most boxes for what we want to see in a laptop of this size. It sports a stunning OLED display, powerful hardware, and a slick design that includes a comfortable keyboard and large glass touchpad. If you don't need a 2-in-1 or a gaming laptop, the Dell XPS 14 is a fantastic machine for work and everyday use.
